stem: In a right triangle, the side opposite acute angle A has length 8, and the non-hypotenuse side adjacent to angle A has length 15. What is the value of tan(A)?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. By definition, tan(A) is the ratio of the side opposite angle A to the side adjacent to angle A (not the hypotenuse): tan(A) = 8/15.

Choice B inverts the ratio (cot A). Choices C and D use the hypotenuse (17 by the Pythagorean theorem), giving sin A and cos A, respectively.
